name: Fan Qiang
page_title: Fan Qiang 范彊 [Shu, Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes: Fan Qiang was a “tent general”, a personal subordinate, of Zhang Fei. Zhang Fei was notorious for physically abusing his soldiers and officers; Fan Qiang was presumably one such victim. Liu Bei warned Zhang Fei not to keep the men that he hurt in his service, but Zhang Fei kept Fan Qiang. When Liu Bei attacked Sun Quan in 221 CE, Zhang Fei was to lead ten thousand men from Langzhong to join him at Jiangzhou. However, Fan Qiang, along with fellow officer Zhang Da, assassinated Zhang Fei. They then took his head and sailed rivers in fleeing to Sun Quan.

sections.short-biography: Assassinated Zhang Fei with Zhang Da, bringing the head to Wu.

full_text:
Fan Qiang 范彊
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Fan Qiang (范彊). Assassinated Zhang Fei with Zhang Da, bringing the head to Wu. Fan Qiang was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom and the Wu Kingdom.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Fan Qiang was a “tent general”, a personal subordinate, of Zhang Fei. Zhang Fei was notorious for physically abusing his soldiers and officers; Fan Qiang was presumably one such victim. Liu Bei warned Zhang Fei not to keep the men that he hurt in his service, but Zhang Fei kept Fan Qiang. When Liu Bei attacked Sun Quan in 221 CE, Zhang Fei was to lead ten thousand men from Langzhong to join him at Jiangzhou. However, Fan Qiang, along with fellow officer Zhang Da, assassinated Zhang Fei. They then took his head and sailed rivers in fleeing to Sun Quan.
